scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope execution advanced through explicit lifecycle states and closed with traceable completion evidence.
Missing jurisdiction, permit, and authority context forced blocked status and low-confidence evidence requests.
Mid-process artifact gaps preserved hold or rework state instead of allowing silent progression.
Scope mismatch and unresolved change handling were routed into exception and rework logic rather than absorbed into normal execution.
Completion without photos, tests, and final inspection evidence was refused with a named evidence gap package.
Permit and site-readiness blockers kept the job from advancing and triggered authority-safe escalation.
Inspection and closeout blockers prevented completion until punch-list, special inspection, and turnover evidence were complete.
Code interpretation and trade signoff were refused and routed to the correct licensed authority.
New safety and inspection failures reversed prior readiness and reopened the exception queue.
Change-order approval and subcontractor scope acceptance were routed out of lane with only execution-impact context retained.
Conflicting tenant and public closeout rules escalated cleanly without fabricated precedence.
Tenant adaptation narrowed packaging and lifecycle handling while preserving public-baseline permit, inspection, and safety guardrails.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, blocked prerequisites, mid-process artifact gaps, rework exceptions, unsupported completion, permit blockers, inspection blockers, authority refusal, and tenant adaptation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the audit contract.
The package is validated as a spec pack but remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
